external_narratives.summary: Geograph article on East Anglia WWII aircrew crash memorials covering the Suffolk section, including the South Cove B-24 Liberator crash. Documents the memorial within the broader collection of crash memorials across the region.
key_details: Part of wider East Anglia crash memorials collection

external_narratives.summary: Wikipedia article on South Cove, Suffolk referencing the B-24 Liberator crash of 409th Bombardment Squadron, 93rd Bombardment Group near the church in March 1945. The churchyard war memorial mentions the loss and a hand-written memorial inside the church lists crew names.
key_details: Hand-written memorial inside church lists crew names
Churchyard war memorial also references the loss

external_narratives.summary: 93rd Bomb Group museum page with B-24 Liberator gallery. The 93rd BG was based at Hardwick and flew Liberators throughout the war, providing unit context for the South Cove crash.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
